China Offshore Intelligent Oilfield Production Monitoring System: Design
and Technical Path Forward for Implementation
Zhong Cheng, Huan Wan, Hongbin Niu, Xiaohui Qu, Xiangxiang Ding, Hongtao Peng, and Yi Zheng, CNOOC Ener
Tech-Drilling &Production Co., Tianjin, China
Copyright 2023, Society of Petroleum Engineers DOI 10.2118/216490-MS
This paper was prepared for presentation at the ADIPEC held in Abu Dhabi, UAE, 2 – 5 October, 2023.
This paper was selected for presentation by an SPE program committee following review of information contained in an abstract submitted by the author(s). Contents
of the paper have not been reviewed by the Society of Petroleum Engineers and are subject to correction by the author(s). The material does not necessarily reflect

Abstract
The advent of the Fourth Industrial Revolution has precipitated a digital metamorphosis in oil companies,
presenting a singular path towards cost reduction and heightened efficiency. As a pivotal direction,
the intelligent oilfield represents an intricate, integrative information system. Its capacity to orchestrate
petroleum exploration, production decisions, and other relevant sectors through data technology facilitates
the standardization and automation of oilfield exploitation. In pursuit of this goal, we propose a novel
digital technology solution specifically designed for offshore oilfields. Harnessing state-of-the-art computer
technologies such as big data, cloud computing, edge computing, optical fiber communication, and IoT, our
aim is to establish an inclusive data integration platform and deliver unified data services to enhance the
efficiency of information utilization.
Within the landscape of the digital oilfield, offshore production monitoring and optimization emerges
as a promising sector where data processing and advanced analytics can be employed to boost revenue,
curtail costs, and mitigate risks. Constructing a data mining and analytics engine to utilize data from
wells, equipment, facilities, and field personnel, however, poses a formidable challenge. The workflow of
data acquisition from offshore production platform groups—comprising main production platforms, sub-
production platforms, transport vessels, and FPSOs—along with the transmission, processing, and timely
analysis of data, are major factors in formulating a data service solution. Consequently, an integrative system
encapsulating all necessary tools can significantly expedite addressing these challenges in a timely manner.
This paper provides a comprehensive technical elucidation of the currently devised intelligent oilfield
production monitoring system. The data service system encompasses four subsystems: (I) Production
management, encompassing remote operation monitoring, production dynamic tracking, reservoir dynamic
management, and well pattern injection and production deployment management; (II) Equipment and
facilities management, which includes equipment health management, submarine pipeline operation and
maintenance management, submarine cable management, and inventory management; (III) Scheduling
management, integrating personnel dynamic dispatching, ship dynamic dispatching, and meteorological and
hydrological monitoring; and (IV) Safety management, ensuring operational safety and personnel status
and location management.

3
The digital construction and intelligent application research and development in the offshore oil
exploration and production industry are reflected in the following four aspects (Ref.2):
1.
The adoption of Industrial IoT technology seamlessly connects production information systems with
oilfield production equipment and facilities, enabling real-time perception of the production process,
automatic data collection, real-time analysis, automatic monitoring, and even automatic control.
This process has facilitated the transition of traditional production methods, significantly improved
production efficiency, and effectively reduced safety production risks.
2.
The application of platformization and cloud platform services has emerged as a new model
for informational construction. By building platforms such as infrastructure cloud, exploration
and production management system, and a geological engineering integrated system, the unified
deployment and shared application of various software and hardware resources are realized.
3.
The construction of enterprise-level data lake projects and the application of big data analysis
techniques have become new methods for companies to enhance their decision-making capabilities.
The data lake integrates different types of data, making many previously unanalyzable data available
for production without predefined models, thus providing technical support for shared analysis of
multi-source heterogeneous data. By big data analysis techniques, users can mine data resources
through association analysis and intelligent methods, intuit and analyze data content, and achieve
precise correlation descriptions of underground oil reservoirs and surface equipment facilities,
enhancing the understanding of oilfield production status and the decision-making support capacity
of the company.
4.
Mobile internet has become a new approach to comprehensively enhance the capability of
informational support services. By utilizing mobile internet technology, the extension of informational
support services in time and spatial is realized, providing mobile application support for oil and gas
field production operations and full-business management at any time and any place.
As a major offshore oil and gas production and sales enterprise in China, CNOOC initiated the planning
study of the "Digital Oilfield" in 2014, completed the construction of a private cloud platform in 2018, and
officially released the "digital transformation top-level design outline" in 2020. This document delineates
the next phase of digital transformation vision: based on centralized and unified data asset management, the
group aims to establish perceptive insights, intelligent control, collaborative sharing, and interconnected
innovative digital capabilities. Building a digital ecosystem that equipment and facilities integration will
create an intelligent offshore oil company. This transformation will enhance the company management
control capabilities, promote cost reduction and efficiency, mitigate significant risks in production and
business management, assist in high-quality development, and achieve a leap from traditional management
models to modern, digital, and intelligent models.
Furthermore, through the deep integration of digital technologies like cloud computing, the IoT, big
data, artificial intelligence, and core exploration and production businesses, the transformation promotes
changes in production methods and optimizes management processes. Based on four typical scenarios,
namely unmanned operation, visualized oil reservoirs, collaborative operations, and scientific decision-
making, the intelligent oilfield application system construction is carried out (Ref.3).
Unmanned operations, primarily targeting production sites, apply digital technologies like IoT, big data,
and artificial intelligence to realize remote monitoring and predictive maintenance. The composite services
of these technologies continually enhance the automation and intelligence levels of major production sites
like offshore platforms and land terminals, reduce the number of personnel at production sites, and promote
changes in production operation methods.

Challenges and problems
Our company began the design and development of an offshore intelligent oilfield production monitoring
system in 2021. As a crucial part of intelligent offshore oilfield construction, this system utilizes industrial
internet technology and data analysis to supervise and enhance offshore oil and gas production performance.
It can provide real-time status information about wells, pipelines, equipment, and processes. Additionally,
it enables remote control and coordination of operations and calculates inferred data not directly measured
by physical instruments, such as virtual meters. This production monitoring system assists operators
and engineers in optimizing production, reducing costs, enhancing safety and reliability, and minimizing
environmental impacts.
However, with the expansion of offshore oil and gas field exploration and production, its model has
gradually shifted from being based on a single oil and gas field (a single platform or multiple platforms)
to multiple oil field regions. This shift has created a connected network of oil, gas, water, and electricity
supply across oil and gas fields. Yet, the production process control systems (PCS, DCS, SCADA, etc.) are
still constructed and operated based on the model of a single platform or a master platform with a number
of satellite platforms. This substantially increases the complexity of the digital system design for oil and
gas production and transportation management. To further reduce the construction cost and development
difficulty of the offshore intelligent oilfield production monitoring system, we adopted an implementation
strategy based on "steady advancement, pilot first, top-level design, comprehensive planning." We selected
the X oil and gas field production operation group in Bohai bay as the pilot construction unit for the
production monitoring digital system.
In terms of production operation equipment and facilities, this operation group includes eight WHP, four
central platforms, an EPP, and one FPSO. It has a relatively independent production and supply pipeline
system. Regarding the fundamental network environment, after network upgrade and expansion, a network
environment supported by fiber optics, microwaves, and satellites was built, providing ample bandwidth
for future data transmission, sharing, and analysis. Concerning data support, the offshore platform central
control systems are highly automated, and many central control subsystems support the universal OPC
protocol. PLC/RTU devices provide the standard Modbus protocol, meeting the requirements for real-time
data processing for oil and gas production and equipment services. Moreover, the company's self-developed
systems based on the C/S structure can satisfy application requirements for oil and gas production and
equipment planning, and dynamic data analysis.
Despite the above advantages and a good foundation for the construction of an offshore intelligent oilfield
production monitoring system in the X oil and gas field production operation group, there are four main
challenges and problems:
1.
Field production data has not yet been integrated into a data lake, affecting real-time access and
the rapid grasp of front-line production conditions by operators. Currently, offshore production and
centralized control real-time data are only preserved separately on various platforms and business
departments, lacking data sharing. Information such as production data, equipment information, and
vessel demands are not collected, summarized, and centralized in a timely and comprehensive manner,
making it challenging to visualize and conveniently view real-time operational dynamics.
2.
Offshore platform oil and gas production management lacks effective automated processes and
business model support, resulting in low operational efficiency. Due to the lack of an integrated
information system for oil and gas production, effective control mechanisms are only reflected in
paper documents, Telephone and email communication. Production management requires information
about well production dynamics, real-time equipment operation status, marine pipe operational
conditions, and equipment facilities archives, among others. These are hard to collect due to their
number and real-time requirements.

SPE-216490-MS
3.
Daily operations lack support from a unified information system platform. Due to the lack of an
information system for support, large amounts of operation data, such as oil and gas production volume
and equipment operating parameters, need to be manually recorded and summarized, making it hard
to ensure data timeliness and quality.
4.
The control systems on offshore platforms, such as central control systems and PLC systems, generally
operate in a closed internal network environment. Production data, video surveillance data, and
power data from offshore platforms are not effectively and persistently stored. The data required
for the production management system is mainly entered manually. There's a lack of an intelligent
application system that provides multi-source data services, making it difficult to meet the actual
needs of production management in terms of data analysis breadth and depth.
System design
System construction objectives
The offshore intelligent oilfield production monitoring system mentioned in this article targets the actual
pain points and difficulties of offshore oil and gas production in the target oil and gas block, focusing on
key breakthroughs. Based on the company's unified data management standards and information system
construction requirements, the construction approach is centered on ‘cloudification + platformization
+ cloud-edge collaboration + agile development and delivery.’ The system development employs the
DevOps model, employing a cloud-edge architecture construction mode and a ‘data + platform + modular
application’ framework.
In combination with actual business needs, the aim is to aid precise decision-making, enhance sea-land
collaboration and production operation efficiency, reduce production costs, and realize the construction
of the industrial internet of offshore oil and gas production. The system primarily achieves the following
objectives:
1.
One platform: A basic operation and maintenance platform is established that suits a unified
environment of cloud-edge collaborative services. It realizes the centralized, unified, and precise
management of information resources, ensures the reasonable allocation of resources, provides a
standardized, secure, and open public service environment for upper-level business applications, and
prevents repetitive software function development to improve IT resource usage efficiency.
2.
Intelligent reservoirs: Production monitoring models, dynamic evaluation models, and production
policy optimization models are created based on the fusion of dynamic and static data of oilfields. This
replaces the traditional manual data collation and various commercial software for data processing
and computation, realizing platform operation for dynamic analysis, indicator evaluation, potential
tapping of oilfields, and adjustment optimization.
3.
Intelligent injection and production: Based on data fusion of injection and production, work condition
monitoring models, evaluation models, and measure optimization models are constructed. So as to
improve the service abilities of the work scenarios such as dynamic monitoring of oil or water well,
abnormal monitoring and analysis of oil or water well working conditions, and formulation of oil and
water treatment measures.
4.
Intelligent production operation: By integrating production data with process flow data, various
models are established, including production monitoring, operation alarm, platform online process
flow, and wellhead virtual metering models.
5.
Predictive maintenance of equipment and facilities: Utilizing a combination of equipment and
facilities archive data, operation data, and production data, we construct fault diagnosis models for
equipment and facilities, as well as equipment health analysis models.

6.
Intelligent safety and environmental protection: Risk alerting models and evaluation models are

System architecture design
The system is built upon the investment in information infrastructure of offshore oil and gas fields. It
revolves around the core assets of oil and gas fields, such as oil reservoirs, oil-water wells, pipelines,
and production facilities. Leveraging deep information technology to assist asset management and benefit
optimization, the system associates reservoir data, production data, and safety monitoring data, thereby
supporting dynamic analysis of reservoir research and production management. It enables two-dimensional
and three-dimensional comprehensive displays of reservoirs, well networks, production processes, and
facilities, thereby building capabilities for real-time perception, integrated coordination, monitoring and
prediction, and analysis and optimization, thus helping in high-quality operation, and maximization of
offshore oilfield asset value.
The design of the system's information architecture focuses on promoting intensive construction,
information sharing, and business collaboration. It comprehensively considers the informatization
development of offshore oil and gas field production, including six aspects of architectural design: overall
architecture, application architecture, technical architecture, data architecture, integration architecture, and
network security architecture.
Overall architecture.   The offshore intelligent oilfield production monitoring system comprehensively
evaluates the unique aspects of both offshore and onshore domains where the edge end implements data
collection and real-time analysis of the monitoring system of offshore platforms, and the cloud end mainly
supports various intelligent business applications. Its overall architecture is shown in Figure 3. To avoid
problems such as unshareable software code, difficult reuse of functional modules, difficult docking of
data resources, and complicated maintenance and upgrade difficulties caused by inconsistencies in software
architecture and development specifications, the concept of "systematic design, modular development,
standardized construction, and integrated assembly" is adopted. A unified technical architecture focusing
on data-driven foundation, and business applications has been established, which puts forward detailed
and clear requirements for software development tools, programming languages, coding standards, graphic
standards, database naming and design specifications, etc. Under the constraints of a unified software
architecture and technical specifications, it can ensure that the future developed software subsystems are
consistent in terms of technical route, development tools, programming language, code standards, data
resources, graphic standards, and interface style. Finally, it realizes continuous expansion of business
functions, lever-type reuse of functional modules, inheritance and sharing of output results, thereby
effectively improving system development efficiency, and promoting the system to continue integration,
operation, and iterative upgrades towards platformization.

combined with downhole parameters, oil and gas field production data, etc., to enhance the visualization
monitoring capabilities of oil and gas production. We are developing risk monitoring technology for
the process of oilfield development and production to achieve monitoring and prediction of risks in
the development and production process, such as indicator changes, production fluctuations, reservoir
management, injection and production allocation. With the oil and gas field development and production
as the main line of business, focusing on reservoir operation management, developing functions such
as dynamic reservoir management, dynamic injection and production management, and coordinated
production process management, establishing a closed-loop management model of production operation
"real-time collection-simulation-model diagnosis-expert decision-remote control", Improve the utilization
degree of oil and gas field reserves, reduce the overall water cut, and slow down the decline in production
(Ref.6).
Reservoir dynamic management.   By integrating various types of geological data and dynamic monitoring
data, applying professional geological reservoir research methods, and linking geological reservoir data,
algorithm models, and analysis results, a comprehensive model that facilitates professional collaborative
research is established. This model adheres to the business logic of well monitoring, single well/well group/
block analysis, remaining oil distribution analysis, production index evaluation, production forecast, and
measures recommendation. This aids in the potential excavation of oil reservoirs, adjustment optimization,
and the enhancement of reservoir management levels (see Figure 10). The main functions are explained
as follows:
1.
Production management: By building oilfield production monitor models, it dynamically tracks the
production change difference. This aids reservoir engineers by ensuring the effective achievement of
annual distribution goals.
2.
Well dynamic analysis: Based on the well dynamic analysis knowledge base and the knowledge graph
technology, well dynamic analysis models are established. These models provide well production
early monitoring and single well potential analysis.
3.
Water injection dynamic analysis: Enables monitor for water injection rates and analysis of individual
well injection potential. This term more accurately reflects the function of monitoring the performance
of water injection, rather than production.
4.
Well group dynamic analysis: Includes injection and production well group oil layer connection status
analysis, well group injection-production balance analysis, and well group remaining oil distribution
status analysis.
5.
Oilfield dynamic analysis: Uses water drive characteristic curves for oilfield yield prediction.
6.
Production monitoring: Provides monitor ability for production indices and production fluctuations
during the production process, aids oil and gas production engineers in completing production analysis
and production trend analysis.

17
helps to formulate scientific maintenance plans, optimize resource allocation, and improve the quality and
efficiency of equipment operation and maintenance. The main functions include:
1.
Moving equipment health management: It statistically analyzes and locates the status monitoring and
fault diagnosis information of the water injection pump, and finally realizes fault statistics and early
warning push with the water injection pump fault diagnosis model.
2.
Static equipment management: It calls up relevant data from the offshore platform static equipment
management system, analyzes the wall thickness thinning situation, cathodic protection potential
information, etc., and graded early warns, alarms, and handles out-of-limit measurement points.
3.
Electrical equipment management: It realizes real-time monitoring and analysis of electrical
equipment such as transformers, medium and high voltage distribution cabinets, and low voltage
distribution boards. It combines electrical equipment fault diagnosis algorithm models for
multidimensional comprehensive evaluation.
4.
Smart instrument management: It monitors the health status of smart instruments online and provides
real-time predictive alarm information. It establishes a smart instrument health diagnosis model to
comprehensively evaluate the health status of smart instruments.
5.
Central control health management: It calls up the real-time diagnostic data of the central control
system equipment, establishes a central control health diagnosis model for hardware diagnosis and
software configuration management of the central control system, and realizes intelligent early
warning and comprehensive diagnosis of the central control system.
Inspection and maintenance management.   This section integrates static data from various systems about
equipment and facilities to manage their inspection and maintenance records, including inspection plans,
multi-dimensional queries for outsourced maintenance, performance indicator statistics, and maintenance
record management. This aids in tracking the entire lifecycle of inspection and testing projects, assisting in
the creation of maintenance plans, optimizing management resources, and providing effective support for
predictive maintenance. Key functions are as follows:
1.
Maintenance plan management: Statistically analyze the execution of inspection plans based on the
type of equipment, inspection type, and planned completion time.
2.
Performance assessment management: Aggregate equipment inspection and maintenance data,
display the annual plan execution status, serviceability ratio, equipment failure impact, statistics of
inspection and maintenance work order information, maintenance costs, etc.
3.
Maintenance record management: Enable querying of detailed maintenance record information and
historical information to enhance the efficiency of offshore user equipment maintenance work.
4.
Inventory management: Implement statistical analysis features for key indicators such as account
inventory, static inventory for three years or more, current year procurement material consumption
rate, inventory aging, and in-transit inventory.
Submarine pipeline operation and maintenance management.   This section involves the use of digital
sea maps to display and dynamically simulate submarine pipeline information. It offers category-wise
statistics, early warning tracing, and visualization interaction, simulates the operation status of pigging, and
reasonably analyzes and optimizes submarine pipeline operation and maintenance solutions. Key functions
are as follows:
1.
Corrosion monitoring management: Analyze corrosion monitoring data (coupon, bypass, FSM), and
give early warnings for exceeding corrosion rates. Display related corrosion monitoring information
of submarine pipeline bypass monitoring, coupon monitoring, FSM monitoring, and analyze corrosion
rate conditions.

SPE-216490-MS
2.
Inspection and maintenance management: Display operation records of internal inspection, pigging
maintenance, and route survey information of submarine pipelines, and statistically analyze pigging,
internal inspection, and external survey data.
3.
Operation and maintenance plan management: Based on the corrosion monitoring and inspection
maintenance module, implement the ultrasonic guided wave monitoring results, evaluate the analysis
results and operation and maintenance plan, and link the monitoring statistics module.
Submarine cable status management.   This section involves analyzing data such as the temperature, current
carrying capacity, disturbance, and strain of submarine cables, introducing a comprehensive assessment
algorithm for submarine cables, establishing a submarine cable assessment system, and assessing the health
status of submarine cables. Key functions include:
1.
Status monitoring management: Display in real time the cable core temperature, ambient temperature,
and working current value of the cable. Call on submarine cable strain monitoring, disturbance
monitoring, current carrying capacity monitoring, and burial depth survey data to monitor, aggregate,
and analyze the operation status of the submarine cable.
2.
Alarm information management: Analyze data such as submarine cable temperature, current carrying
capacity, disturbance, strain, and burial depth from the access system, identify exceeding data, and
give early warnings.
3.
Comprehensive assessment management: Based on the results of submarine cable status monitoring,
perform a comprehensive score for the submarine cable, judge the overall status of the submarine
cable, predict the probability of in-service faults, and determine the in-service risk level.
FPSO operations management.   This section focuses on the business needs of FPSO operation and
production management, improves the safety management level of FPSO through the digital construction
and upgrading of hulls, single-point mooring devices, oil and gas send-out condition. It establishes a
comprehensive monitoring, warning, analysis, recommendation, and execution mechanism. The main
features are as follows:
1.
Hull motion monitoring: Using existing field monitoring equipment, it obtains FPSO hull posture and
hull orientation information, implementing hull posture monitoring and bow direction monitoring.
2.
Distance monitoring between single-point mooring device and FPSO hull: Based on GPS-RTK
system, it monitors the distance between the bow and the single-point mooring device according to
the position of single mooring device.
3.
Mooring leg attitude and rotation angle monitoring: It shows the inclination data of the mooring leg
inclinometer, implementing mooring leg posture monitoring; displays the angle data of the mooring
leg angle meter, realizing the mooring leg rotation angle monitoring.
4.
Oil and gas send-out distance and angle monitoring: It uses a 3D vision matching model algorithm to
monitor and alert of the distance and angle between the FPSO and the shuttle tankers in real-time.
5.
FPSO offloading process monitoring: It displays data such as offloading oil pumps and oil and
offloading flowmeters under oil and gas send-out condition, realizing real-time dynamic monitoring
and analysis of various process data under FPSO offloading.
6.
Dynamic monitoring of FPSO offloading hose pressure: It displays the FPSO offloading hose pressure
under oil and gas send-out condition, realizing real-time dynamic monitoring of FPSO offloading
hose pressure.
Dispatch management
Based on existing systems like the offshore personnel registration system, CNOOC ship dynamic
supervision system, and Bohai bay environment comprehensive service system, it realizes real-time

dispatch information provision for offshore platforms and onshore control centers through functions such
as personnel list statistical analysis, personnel dynamic tracking, maritime vessel position dynamics, real-
time weather forecasting display. Main features are as follows:
1.
Personnel dispatch management: Mainly for personnel statistics, personnel qualification management,

2.
Real-time dynamic management of vessels: Track and count offshore vessels, including duty vessels

Operation safety management
This section uses edge computing devices, image recognition technology, and unmanned inspection
equipment to achieve intelligent identification and alarm control of unsafe human behaviors, unsafe states of
objects, and management vulnerabilities. Combined with electronic approval information of work permits,
it achieves linkage application of video around the work point, risk identification; combined with the
platform's 3D visualization technology, it intuitively displays the distribution and details of operations
in various areas, realizes real-time viewing and process management of on-site operation construction
personnel dynamics, work permits, etc., comprehensively displays and real-time statistics of operation
information, and provides high-risk operation prompts.
Remote monitoring and inspection management.   The remote monitoring and inspection management
system uses technologies such as video surveillance and AI intelligent recognition to alert and locate hazards
such as platform fires and unsafe factors such as not wearing safety helmets. It supports video alarm
record query services, retrieves relevant alarm details, forms a closed-loop management mode of remote
monitoring and collaborative operation, and realizes real-time monitoring warnings, abnormal alarms, and
shutdowns. The main features are as follows:
1.
Video surveillance: Through remote video surveillance, real-time display of live video images is
achieved. Remote video inspections are carried out using video surveillance, real-time display of
on-site monitoring images of various inspection areas on the platform, rapid reporting of abnormal
information, inspection record traces, and assistance in on-site inspections.
2.
Fire probe monitoring: The fire probe is linked to the video surveillance system. When an alarm
occurs, the system immediately retrieves the corresponding video surveillance information for display,
assisting relevant personnel to quickly understand the on-site situation.
3.
Unmanned intelligent inspection: The use of unmanned intelligent inspection equipment realizes
clock-in inspections, one-click upload of abnormal information, reminders for missed inspections, and
ensures that inspection content is traceable, trajectories are followable, abnormalities are reportable,
and overall enhances inspection control capabilities.
Personnel dynamic management.   This section enables platform and individual data display, real-time
display of platform personnel lists, personnel basic information, personnel certificate information, personnel
location information, personnel vital sign information, effectively strengthening the refined management of
offshore crew. The main features are as follows:
1.
Personnel status overview: Real-time collection of personnel vital sign information is achieved
through wearing intelligent monitoring devices equipped with intelligent monitoring bracelets,
realizing health abnormality alarms.

2.
Personnel positioning: Combining personnel positioning with the platform's 3D visualization model,
it realizes real-time dynamic tracking of platform employees, with multiple functions such as batch

Operation construction management.   This section connects key nodes of sea-land construction,
realizes online closed-loop management of outsourced construction from land application to sea
startup, planning, process supervision to completion evaluation, improves sea-land construction planning
management efficiency and on-site operation safety control, and meets the full process standardization and
standardization of construction. The main features are as follows:
1.
Work permit management: Online application and approval of work permits, realizing online and
standardized management of work permits.
2.
Work process visualization: Utilizing personnel positioning and 3D visualization technology, it real-
time displays construction video images and quickly grasps the platform construction operation
situation.
Conclusion
Our research provides a detailed examination of the inaugural stage of an intelligent offshore oilfield
pilot project undertaken by CNOOC in Bohai bay. This trailblazing endeavor is contributing towards an
increased production and a reduction in operational and maintenance expenses by 10% for the oil and
gas field. By fostering an environment conducive to data interoperability and application integration, the
project is propelling the application of advanced technologies such as big data and artificial intelligence in
offshore oil and gas operations. The success of this initiative is setting a precedent for the standardization
and normalization of digital infrastructure within CNOOC, enhancing the enterprise's digital maturity
and intelligent capabilities. Ultimately, these advancements are underpinning CNOOC's high-quality
production, reinforcing its commitment to operational excellence.
In this article, the system architecture design is carried out for the current offshore oil and gas production
business requirements and intelligent function requirements. It studies the management thinking of using the
cloud-edge collaborative system to solve the sea-land linkage of offshore oil and gas field systems, proposes
a new architecture design for the offshore intelligent oil field production monitoring system, supports the
construction of CNOOC's future intelligent oilfield system through this architecture, ensures the robustness
and reliability of the system, and can greatly reduce investment and construction costs.
For CNOOC, it is currently in the research and development exploration stage of intelligent oil field
construction, and intelligence is mostly reflected in intelligent auxiliary management decision-making.
In order to better play the role of informatization innovation technology in the digital transformation of
intelligent oil field management, it is necessary to innovate thinking, use new technology, realize the organic
integration of hardware technology and software technology, provide general software services, integrated
services, operation and maintenance services, and cloud-edge management platform-level services based
on technical capabilities, standards, and capital support, with the goal of deepening business applications,
enhancing cloud-edge security capabilities, and creating a cloud service ecosystem.
